width: 652px; max-width: var(--CYi3c--fRcLaf,652px);"><div style="display: contents;"><div style="font-family: var(--nkmQOe); font-size: 16px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 24px; letter-spacing: 0px; overflow-wrap: break-word; margin-block: var(--xdqYEb--pNrv5d,12px) var(--xdqYEb--i8AQde,16px);"><mark style="color: var(--jINu6c); border-radius: 4px; background: var(--jwFC2e); padding: 0px 2px; font-weight: 500;"><div style="display: contents;">Yes,<span>&nbsp;</span></div><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Flutter</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>and<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">React Native</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>are the dominant cross-platform solutions as of 2026</mark>, collectively accounting for approximately<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">80% of the cross-platform market share</strong>. Flutter currently holds a slight lead with around<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">46% adoption</strong>, while React Native follows with roughly<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">35–40%</strong>.<span><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span>&nbsp;</span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 20px; align-items: center; box-sizing: border-box; overflow: hidden; position: relative; max-inline-size: 250px; vertical-align: text-top; height: 20px; padding: 0px 0px 0px 3px; width: 20px; block-size: 20px; inline-size: auto; padding-block: 3px; padding-inline: 6px; outline: 0px; cursor: pointer;"><div style="align-items: center; display: flex; height: 14px; overflow: hidden; width: 120.133px;"><img id="img-phu7aZH4I7fCkPIPgNrdoAM_1" src="data:image/png;base64," alt="Java Code Geeks" style="background: rgb(255, 255, 255); border-radius: 20px; height: 12px; margin-right: 4px; width: 12px; flex-shrink: 0;"><span style="font-family: &quot;Google Sans&quot;, Roboto, Arial, sans-serif; color: var(--IXoxUe); font-size: 11px; line-height: 1.45; display: flex; overflow: hidden; min-width: 0px; font-weight: 500;"><span style="overflow: hidden; text-overflow: ellipsis; white-space: nowrap; min-width: 0px;">Java Code Geeks</span><span style="flex-shrink: 0;">&nbsp;+2</span></span></div></button></span></span></div><div style="font-family: var(--nkmQOe); font-size: 16px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 24px; letter-spacing: 0px; overflow-wrap: break-word; margin-block: var(--xdqYEb--pNrv5d,12px) var(--xdqYEb--i8AQde,16px);">However, the landscape is diversifying. If you are looking for non-HTML (non-WebView) alternatives that offer native or near-native performance, there are several significant competitors.<span><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span>&nbsp;</span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 10px; height: 20px; padding: 0px; width: 20px; outline: 0px; cursor: pointer;"><span style="color: var(--IXoxUe); display: inline-block; transform: rotate(135deg);"></span></button></span></span></div><div></div><div role="heading" style="margin-block: 24px 12px; position: relative;"><span></span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 10px; height: 20px; padding: 0px; width: 20px; outline: 0px; cursor: pointer;"><span style="color: var(--IXoxUe); display: inline-block; transform: rotate(135deg);"></span></button></span></span></div><ul style="margin: 0px; padding: 0px; margin-block: 12px 16px; font-family: var(--nkmQOe); font-size: 16px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 24px; letter-spacing: 0px; padding-inline-start: 16px;"><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Kotlin Multiplatform (KMP):</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>This is the fastest-growing alternative, reaching<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">23% adoption</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>in 2026.</span><ul style="margin: 0px; padding: 0px; margin-block: 12px 16px; font-family: var(--nkmQOe); font-size: 16px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 24px; letter-spacing: 0px; padding-inline-start: 16px;"><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: circle; padding-inline-start: 0px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">How it works:</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>Unlike Flutter or React Native, KMP primarily shares<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">business logic</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>(networking, data handling) across platforms while allowing you to build truly<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">native UIs</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>using SwiftUI for iOS and Jetpack Compose for Android.</span></li><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: circle; padding-inline-start: 0px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Best for:</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>Teams that want 100% native performance and look-and-feel without duplicating the core "brains" of the app.</span></li></ul></li><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">.NET MAUI (Multi-platform App UI):</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>Microsoft's evolution of Xamarin.</span><ul style="margin: 0px; padding: 0px; margin-block: 12px 16px; font-family: var(--nkmQOe); font-size: 16px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 24px; letter-spacing: 0px; padding-inline-start: 16px;"><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: circle; padding-inline-start: 0px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">How it works:</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>It uses<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">C# and XAML</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>to build native apps for Android, iOS, macOS, and Windows from a single codebase.</span></li><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: circle; padding-inline-start: 0px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Best for:</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>Enterprise environments and teams already heavily invested in the<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Microsoft ecosystem</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>(.NET, Azure, Visual Studio).</span></li></ul></li><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Uno Platform:</strong></span><ul style="margin: 0px; padding: 0px; margin-block: 12px 16px; font-family: var(--nkmQOe); font-size: 16px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 24px; letter-spacing: 0px; padding-inline-start: 16px;"><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: circle; padding-inline-start: 0px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">How it works:</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>A C#-based solution that provides<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">WinUI API compatibility</strong>, allowing developers to target mobile, web (via WebAssembly), desktop (Windows, macOS, Linux), and even embedded systems from one project.</span></li><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: circle; padding-inline-start: 0px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Best for:</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>Organizations needing maximum platform reach, including production-ready<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">WebAssembly and Linux</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>support.</span></li></ul></li><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">NativeScript:</strong></span><ul style="margin: 0px; padding: 0px; margin-block: 12px 16px; font-family: var(--nkmQOe); font-size: 16px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 24px; letter-spacing: 0px; padding-inline-start: 16px;"><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: circle; padding-inline-start: 0px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">How it works:</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>It allows you to use<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">JavaScript or TypeScript</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>(and frameworks like Angular or Vue) to call native APIs directly without a "bridge." Unlike Ionic, it renders<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">platform-native UIs</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>instead of running inside a browser shell.</span></li><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: circle; padding-inline-start: 0px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Best for:</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>Web developers who need deeper, direct access to native mobile APIs than standard hybrid tools provide.</span></li></ul></li><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Unity:</strong></span><ul style="margin: 0px; padding: 0px; margin-block: 12px 16px; font-family: var(--nkmQOe); font-size: 16px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 24px; letter-spacing: 0px; padding-inline-start: 16px;"><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: circle; padding-inline-start: 0px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">How it works:</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>While primarily a game engine, it is increasingly used for non-gaming apps that require<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">3D visualization, AR/VR, or heavy multimedia</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>interactivity.</span></li><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; overflow-wrap: break-word; margin-block: var(--xdqYEb--pNrv5d,12px) var(--xdqYEb--i8AQde,16px);"><div style="display: contents;">While<span>&nbsp;</span></div><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Flutter</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>(Google) and<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">React Native</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>(Meta) currently lead the market, the developers behind the most promising "future-ready" alternatives are focusing on high-performance, native UI, and deep ecosystem integration.<span><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span>&nbsp;</span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 10px; height: 20px; padding: 0px; width: 20px; outline: 0px; cursor: pointer;"><span style="color: var(--IXoxUe); display: inline-block; transform: rotate(135deg);"></span></button></span></span></div><div></div><div role="heading" style="margin-block: 24px 12px; font-family: &quot;Google Sans&quot;, Roboto, Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 20px; font-weight: var(--JT4IPc); line-height: var(--qYGXw); letter-spacing: 0px; color: var(--jINu6c);">The Major Contenders for the Future<span style="visibility: hidden;"><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span></span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 10px; height: 20px; padding: 0px; width: 20px; outline: 0px; cursor: pointer;"><span style="color: var(--IXoxUe); display: inline-block; transform: rotate(135deg);"></span></button></span></span></div><ul style="margin: 0px; padding: 0px; margin-block: 12px 16px; font-family: var(--nkmQOe); font-size: 16px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 24px; letter-spacing: 0px; padding-inline-start: 16px;"><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">JetBrains (Kotlin Multiplatform / KMP):</strong></span><ul style="margin: 0px; padding: 0px; margin-block: 12px 16px; font-family: var(--nkmQOe); font-size: 16px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 24px; letter-spacing: 0px; padding-inline-start: 16px;"><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: circle; padding-inline-start: 0px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">The Vision:</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>JetBrains is positioning KMP as the "modern standard" for sharing business logic while keeping the UI 100% native.</span></li><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: circle; padding-inline-start: 0px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Future Viability:</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>Since its stable release in late 2023, it has gained rapid traction among major companies like<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Netflix</strong>,<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Duolingo</strong>, and<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">McDonald's</strong>. Google officially supports using KMP to share logic between Android and iOS, making it a highly secure bet for the long term.</span></li></ul></li><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Microsoft (.NET MAUI &amp; Uno Platform):</strong></span><ul style="margin: 0px; padding: 0px; margin-block: 12px 16px; font-family: var(--nkmQOe); font-size: 16px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 24px; letter-spacing: 0px; padding-inline-start: 16px;"><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: circle; padding-inline-start: 0px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">The Vision:</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>Microsoft’s<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">.NET MAUI</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>is the successor to Xamarin, designed specifically for enterprise environments already using the Microsoft stack (C#, Azure, Visual Studio).</span></li><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: circle; padding-inline-start: 0px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Future Viability:</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>While it dominates the enterprise sector, it faces competition from the<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Uno Platform</strong>, which uses Microsoft technologies to reach even more platforms, including Linux and production-ready<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">WebAssembly (Wasm)</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>for high-performance web apps.</span></li></ul></li><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">The WebAssembly (Wasm) Movement:</strong></span><ul style="margin: 0px; padding: 0px; margin-block: 12px 16px; font-family: var(--nkmQOe); font-size: 16px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 24px; letter-spacing: 0px; padding-inline-start: 16px;"><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: circle; padding-inline-start: 0px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">The Vision:</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>This isn't a single "dev" but a global browser standard. position: relative;"><span></span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 10px; height: 20px; padding: 0px; width: 20px; outline: 0px; font-family: var(--nkmQOe); font-size: 16px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 24px; letter-spacing: 0px; padding-inline-start: 16px;"><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Flutter (Google):</strong></span><ul style="margin: 0px; padding: 0px; margin-block: 12px 16px; font-family: var(--nkmQOe); font-size: 16px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 24px; letter-spacing: 0px; padding-inline-start: 16px;"><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: circle; padding-inline-start: 0px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Future Outlook:</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>Currently the market leader with approximately<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">46% adoption</strong>.</span></li><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: circle; padding-inline-start: 0px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Why it survives:</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>Its<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Impeller rendering engine</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>is now fully mature, providing pixel-perfect UI at 120 FPS without a "bridge". Google’s deep integration of Flutter into its ecosystem (including AI tools) ensures it remains a standard for the next decade.</span></li></ul></li><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">React Native (Meta):</strong></span><ul style="margin: 0px; padding: 0px; margin-block: 12px 16px; font-family: var(--nkmQOe); font-size: 16px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 24px; letter-spacing: 0px; padding-inline-start: 16px;"><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: circle; padding-inline-start: 0px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Future Outlook:</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>Holds about<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">35% of the market</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>and remains the top choice for teams with JavaScript expertise.</span></li><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: circle; padding-inline-start: 0px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Why it survives:</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>The transition to its<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">New Architecture</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>(Fabric and Turbo Modules) has eliminated the old "bridge" bottleneck, allowing direct communication with native threads and matching the performance of pure native apps.</span></li></ul></li><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Kotlin Multiplatform / KMP (JetBrains):</strong></span><ul style="margin: 0px; padding: 0px; margin-block: 12px 16px; font-family: var(--nkmQOe); font-size: 16px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 24px; letter-spacing: 0px; padding-inline-start: 16px;"><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: circle; padding-inline-start: 0px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Future Outlook:</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>The fastest-growing modern framework, seeing<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">+120% year-over-year growth</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>in enterprise projects.</span></li><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: circle; padding-inline-start: 0px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Why it survives:</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>It is the only major framework that doesn't force a custom UI. It allows you to share<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">business logic</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>(networking, data) while using 100% native UI (SwiftUI/Jetpack Compose). This "pragmatic" approach is being adopted by giants like<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Netflix</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>and<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Disney</strong>.</span><span><span style="white-space: nowrap; padding: 0px; margin-block: 12px 16px; font-family: var(--nkmQOe); font-size: 16px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 24px; letter-spacing: 0px; padding-inline-start: 16px;"><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: circle; padding-inline-start: 0px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Future Outlook:</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>The official successor to Xamarin, specifically for the<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Microsoft ecosystem</strong>.</span></li><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: circle; padding-inline-start: 0px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Why it survives:</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>It is indispensable for enterprise teams already using<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">C# and Azure</strong>. While it has "quirks," it remains the standard for unified Windows/Mobile development in corporate environments.</span></li></ul></li><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Uno Platform:</strong></span><ul style="margin: 0px; padding: 0px; margin-block: 12px 16px; font-family: var(--nkmQOe); font-size: 16px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 24px; letter-spacing: 0px; padding-inline-start: 16px;"><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: circle; padding-inline-start: 0px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Future Outlook:</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>A specialized solution that extends .NET to 6+ platforms, including<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Linux</strong>.</span></li><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: circle; padding-inline-start: 0px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Why it survives:</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>It fills a unique gap by providing a<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Visual Designer</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>and true support for platforms like Linux and WebAssembly (Wasm) that others struggle to reach.</span></li></ul></li><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Unity (Unity Technologies):</strong></span><ul style="margin: 0px; padding: 0px; margin-block: 12px 16px; font-family: var(--nkmQOe); font-size: 16px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 24px; letter-spacing: 0px; padding-inline-start: 16px;"><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: circle; padding-inline-start: 0px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Future Outlook:</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>Dominant in the<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">AR/VR and Gaming</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>space.</span></li><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: circle; padding-inline-start: 0px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Why it survives:</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>As "Super Apps" and immersive experiences (retail, training) grow, Unity remains the only reliable cross-platform choice for high-end 3D and spatial computing.</span><span><span style="white-space: nowrap; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">KMP</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>compiles directly to<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">native binaries</strong>. This means on an iPhone, it is indistinguishable from an app written in Apple's own Swift. color: var(--IXoxUe); font-size: 12px; line-height: 16px; font-weight: 400;"><p style="margin-bottom: 0px;">March 18, 2026</p></div><div></div></div></div><div style="font-family: &quot;Google Sans&quot;, Roboto, Arial, sans-serif; width: 652px; max-width: var(--CYi3c--fRcLaf,652px);"><div style="display: contents;"><div style="font-family: var(--nkmQOe); font-size: 16px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 24px; letter-spacing: 0px; overflow-wrap: break-word; margin-block: var(--xdqYEb--pNrv5d,12px) var(--xdqYEb--i8AQde,16px);"><strong style="font-weight: 700;"><div style="display: contents;">Static Hermes</div></strong><span>&nbsp;</span>is<span>&nbsp;</span><mark style="color: var(--jINu6c); border-radius: 4px; background: var(--jwFC2e); padding: 0px 2px; font-weight: 500;">the next-generation evolution of Meta's Hermes JavaScript engine, designed to compile JavaScript, TypeScript, or Flow code directly into<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">native binary code</strong></mark>. Unlike the standard Hermes engine, which compiles code into bytecode to be interpreted at runtime, Static Hermes performs<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">ahead-of-time (AOT) compilation</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>at build time.<span><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span>&nbsp;</span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 20px; align-items: center; box-sizing: border-box; position: relative;"><span></span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 10px; height: 20px; padding: 0px; width: 20px; outline: 0px; cursor: pointer;"><span style="color: var(--IXoxUe); display: inline-block; transform: rotate(135deg);"></span></button></span></span></div><ul style="margin: 0px; padding: 0px; margin-block: 12px 16px; font-family: var(--nkmQOe); font-size: 16px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 24px; letter-spacing: 0px; padding-inline-start: 16px;"><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Native Binary Generation</strong>: It can transform type-annotated code into native machine code (via C and LLVM), potentially eliminating the need for a JS engine or virtual machine at runtime.</span></li><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Type-Driven Optimization</strong>: It leverages<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">sound type annotations</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>from TypeScript or Flow to apply aggressive optimizations that were previously impossible in dynamic JavaScript.</span></li><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Native Interoperability</strong>: Static Hermes allows for high-performance, direct calls between JavaScript and native C/C++ libraries without the overhead typically associated with bridge layers.</span></li><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Hybrid Execution</strong>: Developers can choose to compile performance-critical sections of an app into native code while keeping the rest as standard Hermes bytecode.</span><span><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span>&nbsp;</span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 20px; align-items: center; box-sizing: border-box; overflow: hidden; position: relative; max-inline-size: 250px; vertical-align: text-top; height: 20px; padding: 0px 0px 0px 3px; width: 20px; block-size: 20px; inline-size: auto; padding-block: 3px; padding-inline: 6px; position: relative;"><span></span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 10px; height: 20px; padding: 0px; width: 20px; outline: 0px; cursor: pointer;"><span style="color: var(--IXoxUe); display: inline-block; transform: rotate(135deg);"></span></button></span></span></div><ul style="margin: 0px; padding: 0px; margin-block: 12px 16px; font-family: var(--nkmQOe); font-size: 16px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 24px; letter-spacing: 0px; padding-inline-start: 16px;"><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Extreme Speed</strong>: Benchmarks have shown specialized tasks improving from<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">1300ms to 120ms</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>just by adding types.</span></li><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Faster Startup</strong>: Because code is pre-compiled into a format the device understands directly, app startup times are significantly reduced.</span></li><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Smaller Footprint</strong>: Highly optimized bytecode and native binaries lead to smaller bundle sizes and reduced memory usage compared to traditional engines like JavaScriptCore or V8.</span><span><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span>&nbsp;</span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 20px; align-items: center; box-sizing: border-box; It is primarily being developed for<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">React Native</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>environments on iOS and Android to replace performance-heavy C++ components with type-safe JavaScript.<span><span style="white-space: nowrap; font-weight: 400; letter-spacing: normal; orphans: 2; text-align: start; text-indent: 0px; text-transform: none; widows: 2; word-spacing: 0px; -webkit-text-stroke-width: 0px; white-space: normal; background-color: rgb(16, 18, 24); text-decoration-thickness: initial; text-decoration-style: initial; text-decoration-color: initial;"><div style="position: relative; padding-bottom: 10px; max-width: 652px; z-index: 1; margin-top: 0px;"><div><div style="display: flex; gap: 4px; margin-left: auto; width: fit-content;"><div style="display: flex; position: relative; overflow: hidden; background-color: var(--XKMDxc); border-radius: 24px 4px 24px 24px; padding: 12px 16px; width: fit-content; flex-direction: column; max-inline-size: 472px; margin-left: auto;"><div style="display: flex; flex-direction: row;"><span role="heading" style="-webkit-box-orient: vertical; -webkit-line-clamp: 2; display: -webkit-box; overflow: hidden; text-overflow: ellipsis; overflow-wrap: break-word; font-family: &quot;Google Sans&quot;, Roboto, Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 16px; line-height: 24px; white-space: pre-wrap;"><span>how proven is it now</span></span></div></div></div><div style="text-align: right; color: var(--IXoxUe); font-size: 12px; line-height: 16px; font-weight: 400;"><p style="margin-bottom: 0px;">March 18, 2026</p></div><div></div></div></div><div style="font-family: &quot;Google Sans&quot;, Roboto, Arial, sans-serif; color: var(--m3c11,#eef0ff); position: relative; font-weight: 400; --m3c9: #e6e8f0; --m3c11: #e6e8f0; display: flex;"><div style="width: 652.438px;"><div><div style="display: contents;"></div></div><div dir="ltr" style="unicode-bidi: isolate; width: 652px; max-width: var(--CYi3c--fRcLaf,652px);"><div style="display: contents;"><div style="font-family: var(--nkmQOe); font-size: 16px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 24px; letter-spacing: 0px; overflow-wrap: break-word; margin-block: var(--xdqYEb--pNrv5d,12px) var(--xdqYEb--i8AQde,16px);"><div style="display: contents;">As of early 2026,<span>&nbsp;</span></div><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Static Hermes</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>is in a transitional phase and is not yet the standard for general production apps. The "Static Hermes" codename has largely been absorbed into the official<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Hermes V1</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>initiative, which is currently<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">experimental</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>in React Native 0.82.<span><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span>&nbsp;</span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 20px; align-items: center; box-sizing: border-box; overflow: hidden; position: relative; max-inline-size: 250px; vertical-align: text-top; height: 20px; padding: 0px 0px 0px 3px; width: 20px; block-size: 20px; inline-size: auto; padding-block: 3px; padding-inline: 6px; outline: 0px; cursor: pointer;"><div style="align-items: center; display: flex; height: 14px; overflow: hidden; width: 76.9141px;"><img id="img-twu_aa_lNqPVkPIP3tX2gQU_5" src="data:image/png;base64," alt="Callstack" style="background: rgb(255, 255, 255); border-radius: 20px; height: 12px; margin-right: 4px; width: 12px; flex-shrink: 0;"><span style="font-family: &quot;Google Sans&quot;, Roboto, Arial, sans-serif; color: var(--IXoxUe); font-size: 11px; line-height: 1.45; display: flex; overflow: hidden; min-width: 0px; font-weight: 500;"><span style="overflow: hidden; text-overflow: ellipsis; white-space: nowrap; min-width: 0px;">Callstack</span><span style="flex-shrink: 0;">&nbsp;+1</span></span></div></button></span></span></div><div></div><div role="heading" style="margin-block: 24px 12px; font-family: &quot;Google Sans&quot;, Roboto, Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 20px; font-weight: var(--JT4IPc); line-height: var(--qYGXw); letter-spacing: 0px; color: var(--jINu6c);">Current Level of "Proven"<span style="visibility: hidden;"><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span></span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 10px; height: 20px; padding: 0px; width: 20px; outline: 0px; cursor: pointer;"><span style="color: var(--IXoxUe); display: inline-block; transform: rotate(135deg);"></span></button></span></span></div><ul style="margin: 0px; padding: 0px; margin-block: 12px 16px; font-family: var(--nkmQOe); font-size: 16px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 24px; letter-spacing: 0px; padding-inline-start: 16px;"><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Experimental Release</strong>: Hermes V1 (the successor incorporating Static Hermes features) was recently announced and requires developers to manually "build from source" to use it.</span></li><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Not the Default</strong>: It is expected to become the stable, default engine in React Native by<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">mid-to-late 2026</strong>.</span></li><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Meta's Internal Usage</strong>: While Meta uses it internally for large-scale apps to gather feedback, it is still considered a "bleeding edge" tool for the broader open-source community.</span><span><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span>&nbsp;</span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 20px; align-items: center; box-sizing: border-box; overflow: hidden; position: relative; max-inline-size: 250px; vertical-align: text-top; height: 20px; padding: 0px 0px 0px 3px; width: 20px; block-size: 20px; inline-size: auto; padding-block: 3px; padding-inline: 6px; outline: 0px; cursor: pointer;"><div style="align-items: center; display: flex; height: 14px; overflow: hidden; width: 73.5078px;"><img id="img-twu_aa_lNqPVkPIP3tX2gQU_20" src="data:image/png;base64," alt="YouTube" style="background: rgb(255, 255, 255); border-radius: 20px; height: 12px; margin-right: 4px; width: 12px; flex-shrink: 0;"><span style="font-family: &quot;Google Sans&quot;, Roboto, Arial, sans-serif; color: var(--IXoxUe); font-size: 11px; line-height: 1.45; display: flex; overflow: hidden; min-width: 0px; font-weight: 500;"><span style="overflow: hidden; text-overflow: ellipsis; white-space: nowrap; min-width: 0px;">YouTube</span><span style="flex-shrink: 0;">&nbsp;+1</span></span></div></button></span></span></li></ul><div></div><div role="heading" style="margin-block: 24px 12px; font-family: &quot;Google Sans&quot;, Roboto, Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 20px; font-weight: var(--JT4IPc); line-height: var(--qYGXw); letter-spacing: 0px; color: var(--jINu6c);">Performance Evidence<span style="visibility: hidden;"><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span></span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 10px; height: 20px; padding: 0px; width: 20px; outline: 0px; cursor: pointer;"><span style="color: var(--IXoxUe); display: inline-block; transform: rotate(135deg);"></span></button></span></span></div><div style="font-family: var(--nkmQOe); font-size: 16px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 24px; letter-spacing: 0px; overflow-wrap: break-word; margin-block: var(--xdqYEb--pNrv5d,12px) var(--xdqYEb--i8AQde,16px);">Early real-world testing (e.g., in the<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">New Expensify</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>app) has shown promising but incremental improvements rather than a total transformation:<span><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span>&nbsp;</span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 10px; height: 20px; padding: 0px; width: 20px; outline: 0px; cursor: pointer;"><span style="color: var(--IXoxUe); display: inline-block; transform: rotate(135deg);"></span></button></span></span></div><ul style="margin: 0px; padding: 0px; margin-block: 12px 16px; font-family: var(--nkmQOe); font-size: 16px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 24px; letter-spacing: 0px; padding-inline-start: 16px;"><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Time to Interactive (TTI)</strong>: Improved by ~2.5% on iOS and ~7.6% on Android.</span></li><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Bundle Load Time</strong>: Reduced by ~9% on iOS and ~3.1% on Android.</span></li><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Specific Operations</strong>: For complex logic like ES6 classes, it is already<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">2.3x to 4.5x faster</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>than standard Babel transforms.</span><span><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span>&nbsp;</span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 20px; align-items: center; box-sizing: border-box; Untyped code currently only sees modest improvements (~10-15%).</span></li><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Build Times</strong>: Enabling these features significantly increases build times because the compiler must perform much more complex native code generation.</span></li><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Tooling Compatibility</strong>: It still has some gaps in JavaScript spec compliance (like missing private fields or static blocks) and some debugging tools may require extra configuration.</span><span><span style="white-space: nowrap; gap: 4px; margin-left: auto; width: fit-content;"><div style="display: flex; position: relative; overflow: hidden; background-color: var(--XKMDxc); border-radius: 24px 4px 24px 24px; padding: 12px 16px; width: fit-content; flex-direction: column; max-inline-size: 472px; margin-left: auto;"><div style="display: flex; flex-direction: row;"><span role="heading" style="-webkit-box-orient: vertical; -webkit-line-clamp: 2; display: -webkit-box; overflow: hidden; text-overflow: ellipsis; overflow-wrap: break-word; font-family: &quot;Google Sans&quot;, Roboto, Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 16px; line-height: 24px; white-space: pre-wrap;"><span>its history</span></span></div></div></div><div style="text-align: right; color: var(--IXoxUe); font-size: 12px; line-height: 16px; font-weight: 400;"><p style="margin-bottom: 0px;">March 18, 2026</p></div><div></div></div></div><div style="font-family: &quot;Google Sans&quot;, Roboto, Arial, sans-serif; color: var(--m3c11,#eef0ff); position: relative; font-weight: 400; --m3c9: #e6e8f0; --m3c11: #e6e8f0; display: flex;"><div style="width: 652.438px;"><div><div style="display: contents;"></div></div><div dir="ltr" style="unicode-bidi: isolate; width: 652px; max-width: var(--CYi3c--fRcLaf,652px);"><div style="display: contents;"><div style="font-family: var(--nkmQOe); font-size: 16px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 24px; letter-spacing: 0px; overflow-wrap: break-word; margin-block: var(--xdqYEb--pNrv5d,12px) var(--xdqYEb--i8AQde,16px);"><div style="display: contents;">Static Hermes is the result of a multi-year effort by Meta to solve the "JIT problem" on mobile devices, specifically iOS, where Just-In-Time compilation is restricted for security reasons</div>.<span><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span>&nbsp;</span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 20px; align-items: center; box-sizing: border-box; overflow: hidden; position: relative; max-inline-size: 250px; vertical-align: text-top; height: 20px; padding: 0px 0px 0px 3px; width: 20px; block-size: 20px; inline-size: auto; padding-block: 3px; padding-inline: 6px; outline: 0px; cursor: pointer;"><div style="align-items: center; display: flex; height: 14px; overflow: hidden; width: 63.0703px;"><img id="img-twu_aa_lNqPVkPIP3tX2gQU_1" src="data:image/png;base64," alt="Reddit" style="background: rgb(255, 255, 255); border-radius: 20px; height: 12px; margin-right: 4px; width: 12px; flex-shrink: 0;"><span style="font-family: &quot;Google Sans&quot;, Roboto, Arial, sans-serif; color: var(--IXoxUe); font-size: 11px; line-height: 1.45; display: flex; overflow: hidden; min-width: 0px; font-weight: 500;"><span style="overflow: hidden; text-overflow: ellipsis; white-space: nowrap; min-width: 0px;">Reddit</span><span style="flex-shrink: 0;">&nbsp;+1</span></span></div></button></span></span></div><div></div><div role="heading" style="margin-block: 24px 12px; font-family: &quot;Google Sans&quot;, Roboto, Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 20px; font-weight: var(--JT4IPc); line-height: var(--qYGXw); letter-spacing: 0px; color: var(--jINu6c);">Timeline of Evolution<span style="visibility: hidden;"><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span></span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 10px; height: 20px; padding: 0px; width: 20px; outline: 0px; cursor: pointer;"><span style="color: var(--IXoxUe); display: inline-block; transform: rotate(135deg);"></span></button></span></span></div><ul style="margin: 0px; padding: 0px; margin-block: 12px 16px; font-family: var(--nkmQOe); font-size: 16px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 24px; letter-spacing: 0px; padding-inline-start: 16px;"><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">2019: The Birth of Hermes</strong>: Meta<span>&nbsp;</span><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;">open-sourced Hermes</span><span>&nbsp;</span>at Chain React, focusing on Ahead-of-Time (AOT) bytecode compilation to improve Android startup times.</span></li><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">2022: Becoming the Standard</strong>: Hermes became the<span>&nbsp;</span><span><a rel="noopener" href="https://retool.com/blog/hermes-react-native-same-league-swift-kotlin" style="color: var(--JKqx2); text-decoration-line: underline; text-decoration-thickness: 1px; text-decoration-style: initial; text-decoration-color: var(--JKqx2); -webkit-tap-highlight-color: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.1); text-underline-offset: 1px; font-weight: 500; outline: 0px;">default engine for React Native</a></span>, replacing JavaScriptCore (JSC).</span></li><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">2023: Static Hermes Announcement</strong>: At React Native EU 2023, lead engineer Tzvetan Mikov<span>&nbsp;</span><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;">unveiled "Static Hermes"</span>, an experimental project to compile typed JavaScript (TypeScript/Flow) directly into native machine code.</span></li><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">2024: Development in the Open</strong>: Throughout 2024, the team worked on the<span>&nbsp;</span><code dir="ltr" style="unicode-bidi: isolate; font-size: 14px; line-height: 22px; background-color: var(--XKMDxc); border: 1px solid var(--XKMDxc); border-radius: 4px; padding: 2px 4px;">static_h</code><span>&nbsp;</span>branch of the<span>&nbsp;</span><span><a rel="noopener" href="https://github.com/facebook/hermes/blob/static_h/doc/blog/README.md" style="color: var(--JKqx2); text-decoration-line: underline; text-decoration-thickness: 1px; text-decoration-style: initial; text-decoration-color: var(--JKqx2); -webkit-tap-highlight-color: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.1); text-underline-offset: 1px; font-weight: 500; outline: 0px;">official Hermes repository</a></span>, adding support for sound types, unions, and direct C interop.</span></li><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Late 2025: Transition to Hermes V1</strong>: The "Static Hermes" codename was retired in favor of<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Hermes V1</strong>, which began shipping these experimental performance foundations in<span>&nbsp;</span><span><a rel="noopener" href="https://medium.com/react-native-journal/hermes-v1-in-react-native-0-82-unlocking-faster-startup-times-bfd0cf1b107c" style="color: var(--JKqx2); text-decoration-line: underline; text-decoration-thickness: 1px; text-decoration-style: initial; text-decoration-color: var(--JKqx2); -webkit-tap-highlight-color: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.1); text-underline-offset: 1px; font-weight: 500; outline: 0px;">React Native 0.82</a></span>.</span><span><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span>&nbsp;</span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 10px; height: 20px; padding: 0px; width: 20px; outline: 0px; cursor: pointer;"><span style="color: var(--IXoxUe); display: inline-block; transform: rotate(135deg);"></span></button></span></span></li></ul><div></div><div role="heading" style="margin-block: 24px 12px; font-family: &quot;Google Sans&quot;, Roboto, Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 20px; font-weight: var(--JT4IPc); line-height: var(--qYGXw); letter-spacing: 0px; color: var(--jINu6c);">Core Shift: Bytecode vs. Native<span style="visibility: hidden;"><span style="white-space: nowrap; padding: 0px; list-style: decimal;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Static Hermes (V1)</strong>: Takes Typed JS → Compiles to<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">C/LLVM</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>→ Generates<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Native Binary</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>at build time → Runs directly on the device hardware.</span><span><span style="white-space: nowrap; This led the team to develop<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Sound Types</strong>, a stricter subset of types that the compiler can actually trust to generate optimized machine code.<span><span style="white-space: nowrap; text-decoration-thickness: initial; text-decoration-style: initial; text-decoration-color: initial;"><div style="position: relative; padding-bottom: 10px; max-width: 652px; z-index: 1; margin-top: 0px;"><div><div style="display: flex; gap: 4px; margin-left: auto; width: fit-content;"><div style="display: flex; position: relative; overflow: hidden; background-color: var(--XKMDxc); border-radius: 24px 4px 24px 24px; padding: 12px 16px; width: fit-content; flex-direction: column; max-inline-size: 472px; margin-left: auto;"><div style="display: flex; flex-direction: row;"><span role="heading" style="-webkit-box-orient: vertical; -webkit-line-clamp: 2; display: -webkit-box; overflow: hidden; text-overflow: ellipsis; overflow-wrap: break-word; font-family: &quot;Google Sans&quot;, Roboto, Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 16px; line-height: 24px; white-space: pre-wrap;"><span>how much of js is it gonna support. how popular is hermes among ppl.</span></span></div></div></div><div style="text-align: right; color: var(--IXoxUe); font-size: 12px; line-height: 16px; font-weight: 400;"><p style="margin-bottom: 0px;">March 18, 2026</p></div><div></div></div></div><div style="font-family: &quot;Google Sans&quot;, Roboto, Arial, sans-serif; color: var(--m3c11,#eef0ff); position: relative; font-weight: 400; --m3c9: #e6e8f0; --m3c11: #e6e8f0; display: flex;"><div style="width: 652.438px;"><div><div style="display: contents;"></div></div><div dir="ltr" style="unicode-bidi: isolate; width: 652px; max-width: var(--CYi3c--fRcLaf,652px);"><div style="display: contents;"><div style="font-family: var(--nkmQOe); font-size: 16px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 24px; letter-spacing: 0px; overflow-wrap: break-word; margin-block: var(--xdqYEb--pNrv5d,12px) var(--xdqYEb--i8AQde,16px);"><div style="display: contents;">As of early 2026, Hermes has shifted from a specialized tool to the dominant industry standard for mobile JavaScript, while its "static" capabilities are being integrated into the core engine to broaden its support for modern language features.</div><span style="visibility: hidden;"><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span></span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 10px; height: 20px; padding: 0px; width: 20px; outline: 0px; cursor: pointer;"><span style="color: var(--IXoxUe); display: inline-block; transform: rotate(135deg);"></span></button></span></span></div><div></div><div role="heading" style="margin-block: 24px 12px; font-family: &quot;Google Sans&quot;, Roboto, Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 20px; font-weight: var(--JT4IPc); line-height: var(--qYGXw); letter-spacing: 0px; color: var(--jINu6c);">JavaScript Support<span style="visibility: hidden;"><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span></span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 10px; height: 20px; padding: 0px; width: 20px; outline: 0px; cursor: pointer;"><span style="color: var(--IXoxUe); display: inline-block; transform: rotate(135deg);"></span></button></span></span></div><div style="font-family: var(--nkmQOe); font-size: 16px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 24px; letter-spacing: 0px; overflow-wrap: break-word; margin-block: var(--xdqYEb--pNrv5d,12px) var(--xdqYEb--i8AQde,16px);">Hermes is designed for<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">ECMAScript compliance</strong>, rather than full Node.js or browser API compatibility. The release of<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Hermes V1</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>(the evolution of Static Hermes) has significantly expanded its native support for modern JS:<span><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span>&nbsp;</span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 10px; height: 20px; padding: 0px; width: 20px; outline: 0px; cursor: pointer;"><span style="color: var(--IXoxUe); display: inline-block; transform: rotate(135deg);"></span></button></span></span></div><ul style="margin: 0px; padding: 0px; margin-block: 12px 16px; font-family: var(--nkmQOe); font-size: 16px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 24px; letter-spacing: 0px; padding-inline-start: 16px;"><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Core Language</strong>: It now natively supports<span>&nbsp;</span><code dir="ltr" style="unicode-bidi: isolate; font-size: 14px; line-height: 22px; background-color: var(--XKMDxc); border: 1px solid var(--XKMDxc); border-radius: 4px; padding: 2px 4px;">let</code>,<span>&nbsp;</span><code dir="ltr" style="unicode-bidi: isolate; font-size: 14px; line-height: 22px; background-color: var(--XKMDxc); border: 1px solid var(--XKMDxc); border-radius: 4px; padding: 2px 4px;">const</code>,<span>&nbsp;</span><code dir="ltr" style="unicode-bidi: isolate; font-size: 14px; line-height: 22px; background-color: var(--XKMDxc); border: 1px solid var(--XKMDxc); border-radius: 4px; padding: 2px 4px;">async/await</code>, and<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">ES6 Classes</strong>.</span></li><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Built-in Objects</strong>: Features like<span>&nbsp;</span><code dir="ltr" style="unicode-bidi: isolate; font-size: 14px; line-height: 22px; background-color: var(--XKMDxc); border: 1px solid var(--XKMDxc); border-radius: 4px; padding: 2px 4px;">Map</code>,<span>&nbsp;</span><code dir="ltr" style="unicode-bidi: isolate; font-size: 14px; line-height: 22px; background-color: var(--XKMDxc); border: 1px solid var(--XKMDxc); border-radius: 4px; padding: 2px 4px;">Set</code>,<span>&nbsp;</span><code dir="ltr" style="unicode-bidi: isolate; font-size: 14px; line-height: 22px; background-color: var(--XKMDxc); border: 1px solid var(--XKMDxc); border-radius: 4px; padding: 2px 4px;">Symbol</code>, and<span>&nbsp;</span><code dir="ltr" style="unicode-bidi: isolate; font-size: 14px; line-height: 22px; background-color: var(--XKMDxc); border: 1px solid var(--XKMDxc); border-radius: 4px; padding: 2px 4px;">Proxy</code><span>&nbsp;</span>are now handled natively by the engine, which reduces the need for heavy "polyfills" and results in smaller app sizes.</span></li><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Static/Typed Mode</strong>: While it is "fully compatible" with standard JS, its high-performance<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Typed Mode</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>is stricter. It currently struggles with highly dynamic patterns like those found in the<span>&nbsp;</span><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;">Effect Framework</span><span>&nbsp;</span>(e.g., private class fields or computed property names).</span></li><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">WebAssembly (WASM)</strong>: A major 2026 update added<span>&nbsp;</span><span><a rel="noopener" href="https://www.callstack.com/newsletters/react-native-evals-expo-sdk-55-and-webassembly-in-hermes" style="color: var(--JKqx2); text-decoration-line: underline; text-decoration-thickness: 1px; text-decoration-style: initial; text-decoration-color: var(--JKqx2); -webkit-tap-highlight-color: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.1); text-underline-offset: 1px; font-weight: 500; outline: 0px;">native WebAssembly support</a></span>, allowing developers to run C++, Rust, or Go code directly within Hermes.</span><span><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span>&nbsp;</span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 20px; align-items: center; box-sizing: border-box; overflow: hidden; position: relative; max-inline-size: 250px; vertical-align: text-top; position: relative;"><span>&nbsp;</span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 10px; height: 20px; padding: 0px; width: 20px; font-family: var(--nkmQOe); font-size: 16px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 24px; letter-spacing: 0px; padding-inline-start: 16px;"><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Universal Default</strong>: As of<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">React Native 0.84 (February 2026)</strong>, Hermes V1 is the default engine for all new projects on both iOS and Android.</span></li><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Ecosystem Integration</strong>: It is the<span>&nbsp;</span><span><a rel="noopener" href="https://docs.expo.dev/guides/using-hermes/" style="color: var(--JKqx2); text-decoration-line: underline; text-decoration-thickness: 1px; text-decoration-style: initial; text-decoration-color: var(--JKqx2); -webkit-tap-highlight-color: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.1); text-underline-offset: 1px; font-weight: 500; outline: 0px;">standard engine for Expo</a></span>, which powers a massive portion of the React Native community.</span></li><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">High-Volume Apps</strong>: It is proven at scale by major tech companies including<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Meta</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>(Facebook, Instagram),<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Discord</strong>,<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Expensify</strong>, and<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Microsoft</strong>.</span></li><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; overflow-wrap: break-word; margin-block: var(--xdqYEb--pNrv5d,12px) var(--xdqYEb--i8AQde,16px);"><div style="display: contents;">Static Hermes does not try to "fix" JavaScript's design errors; instead, it provides a way to<span>&nbsp;</span></div><strong style="font-weight: 700;">bypass</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>them for performance-critical sections of your code while remaining fully compatible with standard JavaScript.<span><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span>&nbsp;</span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 20px; align-items: center; box-sizing: border-box; overflow: hidden; position: relative; max-inline-size: 250px; vertical-align: text-top; height: 20px; padding: 0px 0px 0px 3px; width: 20px; block-size: 20px; inline-size: auto; padding-block: 3px; padding-inline: 6px; outline: 0px; cursor: pointer;"><div style="align-items: center; display: flex; height: 14px; overflow: hidden; width: 67.7734px;"><img id="img-twu_aa_lNqPVkPIP3tX2gQU_7" src="data:image/png;base64," alt="GitHub" style="background: rgb(255, 255, 255); border-radius: 20px; height: 12px; margin-right: 4px; width: 12px; flex-shrink: 0;"><span style="font-family: &quot;Google Sans&quot;, Roboto, Arial, sans-serif; color: var(--IXoxUe); font-size: 11px; line-height: 1.45; display: flex; overflow: hidden; min-width: 0px; font-weight: 500;"><span style="overflow: hidden; text-overflow: ellipsis; white-space: nowrap; min-width: 0px;">GitHub</span><span style="flex-shrink: 0;">&nbsp;+2</span></span></div></button></span></span></div><div></div><div role="heading" style="margin-block: 24px 12px; font-family: &quot;Google Sans&quot;, Roboto, Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 20px; font-weight: var(--JT4IPc); line-height: var(--qYGXw); letter-spacing: 0px; color: var(--jINu6c);">Handling Untyped Code and JSON<span style="visibility: hidden;"><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span></span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 10px; height: 20px; padding: 0px; width: 20px; outline: 0px; cursor: pointer;"><span style="color: var(--IXoxUe); display: inline-block; transform: rotate(135deg);"></span></button></span></span></div><div style="font-family: var(--nkmQOe); font-size: 16px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 24px; letter-spacing: 0px; overflow-wrap: break-word; margin-block: var(--xdqYEb--pNrv5d,12px) var(--xdqYEb--i8AQde,16px);">You do not need to build or use a separate interpreter. Static Hermes is a<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">hybrid engine</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>that manages different types of code simultaneously:<span><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span>&nbsp;</span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 20px; align-items: center; box-sizing: border-box; overflow: hidden; position: relative; max-inline-size: 250px; vertical-align: text-top; height: 20px; padding: 0px 0px 0px 3px; width: 20px; block-size: 20px; inline-size: auto; padding-block: 3px; padding-inline: 6px; outline: 0px; cursor: pointer;"><div style="align-items: center; display: flex; height: 14px; overflow: hidden; width: 73.5078px;"><img id="img-twu_aa_lNqPVkPIP3tX2gQU_56" src="data:image/png;base64," alt="YouTube" style="background: rgb(255, 255, 255); border-radius: 20px; height: 12px; margin-right: 4px; width: 12px; flex-shrink: 0;"><span style="font-family: &quot;Google Sans&quot;, Roboto, Arial, sans-serif; color: var(--IXoxUe); font-size: 11px; line-height: 1.45; display: flex; overflow: hidden; min-width: 0px; font-weight: 500;"><span style="overflow: hidden; text-overflow: ellipsis; white-space: nowrap; min-width: 0px;">YouTube</span><span style="flex-shrink: 0;">&nbsp;+1</span></span></div></button></span></span></div><ul style="margin: 0px; padding: 0px; margin-block: 12px 16px; font-family: var(--nkmQOe); font-size: 16px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 24px; letter-spacing: 0px; padding-inline-start: 16px;"><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Untyped Mode (Standard JS)</strong>: You can absolutely disable "Typed Mode" or simply not use types. In this case, Static Hermes acts as an<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">AOT-compiled interpreter</strong>. It compiles your JavaScript into a native binary that still preserves all dynamic JS behaviors (like adding properties to objects at runtime).</span></li><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">JSON Support</strong>: JSON is handled just like it is in any other JS engine.</span><ul style="margin: 0px; padding: 0px; margin-block: 12px 16px; font-family: var(--nkmQOe); font-size: 16px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 24px; letter-spacing: 0px; padding-inline-start: 16px;"><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: circle; padding-inline-start: 0px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Parsing</strong>: You use<span>&nbsp;</span><code dir="ltr" style="unicode-bidi: isolate; font-size: 14px; line-height: 22px; background-color: var(--XKMDxc); border: 1px solid var(--XKMDxc); border-radius: 4px; padding: 2px 4px;">JSON.parse()</code>. Because JSON results in dynamic, untyped objects, the engine treats the resulting data as<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">untyped mode</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>objects.</span></li><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: circle; padding-inline-start: 0px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Performance</strong>: While standard JSON literals in<span>&nbsp;</span><code dir="ltr" style="unicode-bidi: isolate; font-size: 14px; line-height: 22px; background-color: var(--XKMDxc); border: 1px solid var(--XKMDxc); border-radius: 4px; padding: 2px 4px;">.js</code><span>&nbsp;</span>files historically had some compiler bugs in Hermes, using<span>&nbsp;</span><code dir="ltr" style="unicode-bidi: isolate; font-size: 14px; line-height: 22px; background-color: var(--XKMDxc); border: 1px solid var(--XKMDxc); border-radius: 4px; padding: 2px 4px;">JSON.parse()</code><span>&nbsp;</span>remains the recommended and safe way to handle large data sets.</span></li></ul></li><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Interoperability</strong>: You can mix both modes in the same app. For example, your high-performance math logic could be in<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Typed Mode</strong>, while your data-fetching and JSON-parsing logic stays in<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Untyped Mode</strong>.</span><span><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span>&nbsp;</span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 20px; align-items: center; box-sizing: border-box; overflow: hidden; position: relative; max-inline-size: 250px; vertical-align: text-top; height: 20px; padding: 0px 0px 0px 3px; width: 20px; block-size: 20px; inline-size: auto; padding-block: 3px; padding-inline: 6px; outline: 0px; cursor: pointer;"><div style="align-items: center; display: flex; height: 14px; overflow: hidden; width: 68.0078px;"><img id="img-twu_aa_lNqPVkPIP3tX2gQU_259" src="data:image/png;base64," alt="GitHub" style="background: rgb(255, 255, 255); border-radius: 20px; height: 12px; margin-right: 4px; width: 12px; flex-shrink: 0;"><span style="font-family: &quot;Google Sans&quot;, Roboto, Arial, sans-serif; color: var(--IXoxUe); font-size: 11px; line-height: 1.45; display: flex; overflow: hidden; min-width: 0px; font-weight: 500;"><span style="overflow: hidden; text-overflow: ellipsis; white-space: nowrap; min-width: 0px;">GitHub</span><span style="flex-shrink: 0;">&nbsp;+6</span></span></div></button></span></span></li></ul><div></div><div role="heading" style="margin-block: 24px 12px; font-family: &quot;Google Sans&quot;, Roboto, Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 20px; font-weight: var(--JT4IPc); line-height: var(--qYGXw); letter-spacing: 0px; color: var(--jINu6c);">The "Two-Engine" Illusion<span style="visibility: hidden;"><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span></span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 10px; height: 20px; padding: 0px; width: 20px; outline: 0px; cursor: pointer;"><span style="color: var(--IXoxUe); display: inline-block; transform: rotate(135deg);"></span></button></span></span></div><div style="font-family: var(--nkmQOe); font-size: 16px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 24px; letter-spacing: 0px; overflow-wrap: break-word; margin-block: var(--xdqYEb--pNrv5d,12px) var(--xdqYEb--i8AQde,16px);">Technically, there aren't two separate interpreters. There is one<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">unified compiler pipeline</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>that decides how to transform your code based on whether you've provided "sound" types:<span><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span>&nbsp;</span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 20px; align-items: center; box-sizing: border-box; overflow: hidden; position: relative; max-inline-size: 250px; vertical-align: text-top; height: 20px; padding: 0px 0px 0px 3px; width: 20px; block-size: 20px; inline-size: auto; padding-block: 3px; padding-inline: 6px; outline: 0px; cursor: pointer;"><div style="align-items: center; display: flex; height: 14px; overflow: hidden; width: 67.7734px;"><img id="img-twu_aa_lNqPVkPIP3tX2gQU_285" src="data:image/png;base64," alt="GitHub" style="background: rgb(255, 255, 255); border-radius: 20px; height: 12px; margin-right: 4px; width: 12px; flex-shrink: 0;"><span style="font-family: &quot;Google Sans&quot;, Roboto, Arial, sans-serif; color: var(--IXoxUe); font-size: 11px; line-height: 1.45; display: flex; overflow: hidden; min-width: 0px; font-weight: 500;"><span style="overflow: hidden; text-overflow: ellipsis; white-space: nowrap; min-width: 0px;">GitHub</span><span style="flex-shrink: 0;">&nbsp;+2</span></span></div></button></span></span></div><ol style="margin: 0px; padding: 0px; margin-block: 12px 16px; font-family: var(--nkmQOe); font-size: 16px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 24px; letter-spacing: 0px; padding-inline-start: 20px;"><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: decimal;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">With Sound Types</strong>: It generates highly optimized, direct machine code (similar to C++).</span></li><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: decimal;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Without Sound Types</strong>: It generates native code that implements a "virtual machine" loop, essentially embedding the interpreter's logic into the binary itself.</span><span><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span>&nbsp;</span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 20px; align-items: center; box-sizing: border-box; overflow: hidden; position: relative; max-inline-size: 250px; vertical-align: text-top; height: 20px; padding: 0px 0px 0px 3px; width: 20px; block-size: 20px; inline-size: auto; padding-block: 3px; padding-inline: 6px; outline: 0px; cursor: pointer;"><div style="align-items: center; display: flex; height: 14px; overflow: hidden; width: 67.6562px;"><img id="img-twu_aa_lNqPVkPIP3tX2gQU_298" src="data:image/png;base64," alt="GitHub" style="background: rgb(255, 255, 255); border-radius: 20px; height: 12px; margin-right: 4px; width: 12px; flex-shrink: 0;"><span style="font-family: &quot;Google Sans&quot;, Roboto, Arial, sans-serif; color: var(--IXoxUe); font-size: 11px; line-height: 1.45; display: flex; overflow: hidden; min-width: 0px; font-weight: 500;"><span style="overflow: hidden; text-overflow: ellipsis; white-space: nowrap; min-width: 0px;">GitHub</span><span style="flex-shrink: 0;">&nbsp;+3</span></span></div></button></span></span></li></ol><div style="font-family: var(--nkmQOe); font-size: 16px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 24px; letter-spacing: 0px; overflow-wrap: break-word; margin-block: var(--xdqYEb--pNrv5d,12px) var(--xdqYEb--i8AQde,16px);">This approach allows you to "pay only for what you use." If you don't use types, you get the standard (but still fast) Hermes experience.<span><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span>&nbsp;</span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px;
